Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Gilbert coordinates the station's news coverage. "We carry NPR for national and international news so our efforts are focused on news in central Pennsylvania, namely the Harrisburg-Lancaster-York- Reading-Sunbury region," he says. "There are two big misconceptions among PR people. Number one is that we cover national news and number two is that we'd even think about airing an audio news release or prepared report. That stuff isn't news, only propaganda and as such it has no place on the airwaves of a radio or television station that considers it a credible source of news," he believes. Email pitches to news@witf.org. No calls.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> As an anchor on an all news radio station, Nikazy covers breaking stories of the day including crime, war, politics, entertainment and sports. "The biggest misunderstanding among those in PR is limitations on air time," he says. "For example, we have a reporter who interviews authors for 45 second reports. He conducts a 5 to 10 minute interview, but is only able to use a few ten second clips." Availability of newsmakers on the weekends or after hours is helpful. Best to contact Dee Patel, the station's news director, who assigns stories.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Wants story ideas with a "different spin on 'today's story.'" Also tell him how the story relates to Pittsburgh. Although they have no full-time business reporter, "We are dedicated to business news coverage," he says. Pitches that are "too commercial" will land in the trash. Hooking up with a non-profit group for an event or promotion will help spark his interest. Potential guests should be "knowledgeable" and "glib" as interview segments are brief. Mail or fax a press kit, release, story pitch or backgrounder with a week's lead time, but remember that final assignments are made only on the day of the event. Gottlieb notes that they usually don't do books and authors unless it's extremely topical. Best contact times are Mon.-Thu. mornings; only call for breaking news.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Gilbert coordinates the station's news coverage. "We carry NPR for national and international news so our efforts are focused on news in central Pennsylvania, namely the Harrisburg-Lancaster-York- Reading-Sunbury region," he says. "There are two big misconceptions among PR people. Number one is that we cover national news and number two is that we'd even think about airing an audio news release or prepared report. That stuff isn't news, only propaganda and as such it has no place on the airwaves of a radio or television station that considers it a credible source of news," he believes. Email pitches to news@witf.org. No calls.
